title: Which Of The Following Is Not Cui Cyber Awareness
source_filename: Which Of The Following Is Not Cui Cyber Awareness.docx

qas.q:
Which of the following is true of protecting classified data?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Classified material must be appropriately marked.

qas.q:
A vendor conducting a pilot program with your organization contacts you for organizational data to use in a prototype. How should you respond?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Refer the vendor to the appropriate personnel.

qas.q:
When classified data is not in use, how can you protect it?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Store classified data appropriately in a GSA-approved vault/container.

qas.q:
What is the basis for handling and storage of classified data?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Classification markings and handling caveats.

qas.q:
Which of the following is a good practice to protect classified information?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Don't assume open storage in a secure facility is permitted.

qas.q:
Which of the following must you do before using an unclassified laptop and peripherals in a collateral classified environment?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Ensure that any cameras, microphones, and Wi-Fi embedded in the laptop are physically disabled.

qas.q:
What level of damage to national security can you reasonably expect Top secret information to cause if disclosed?
(CLASSIFIED DATA)
qas.a: Exceptionally grave damage.

qas.q:
How should you secure your home wireless network for teleworking?
(HOME COMPUTER SECURITY)
qas.a: Implement Wi-Fi Protected Access 2 (WPA2) Personal encryption at a minimum.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true about telework?
(HOME COMPUTER SECURITY)
qas.a: You must have your organization's permission to telework.

qas.q:
Which of the following is a reportable insider threat activity?
(INSIDER THREAT)
qas.a: Attempting to access sensitive information without need-to-know.

qas.q:
Which scenario might indicate a reportable insider threat?
(INSIDER THREAT)
qas.a: A colleague removes sensitive information without seeking authorization in order to perform authorized telework.

qas.q:
Which of the following is a potential insider threat indicator?
(INSIDER THREAT)
qas.a:
1) Unusual interest in classified information.

2) Difficult life circumstances, such as death of spouse.

qas.q:
Based on the description that follows, how many potential insider threat indicator(s) are displayed?
A colleague saves money for an overseas vacation every year, is a single father, and occasionally consumes alcohol.
(INSIDER THREAT)
qas.a: 0 indicators.

qas.q:
Which piece of information is safest to include on your social media profile?
(SOCIAL NETWORKING)
qas.a: Your favorite movie.

qas.q:
Which of the following statements is true?
(SOCIAL NETWORKING)
qas.a:
1) Many apps and smart devices collect and share your personal information and contribute to your online identity.

2) Adversaries exploit social networking sites to disseminate fake news.

qas.q:
How can you protect your organization on social networking sites?
(SOCIAL NETWORKING)
qas.a: Ensure there are no identifiable landmarks visible in any photos taken in a work setting that you post.

qas.q:
Which designation marks information that does not have potential to damage national security?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Unclassified.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true of Unclassified information?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: It is releasable to the public without clearance.??

qas.q:
Which is a best practice for protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Store it in a locked desk drawer after working hours.

qas.q:
Which of the following best describes a way to safely transmit Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Paul verifies that the information is CUI, includes a CUI marking in the subject header, and digitally signs an e-mail containing CUI.

qas.q:
Which designation includes Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and Protected Health Information (PHI)?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI).

qas.q:
Which of the following is NOT an example of CUI?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Press release data.

qas.q:
Which of the following is NOT a correct way to protect CUI?
(CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION)
qas.a: CUI may be stored on any password-protected system.

qas.q:
Which of the following best describes good physical security?
(PHYSICAL SECURITY)
qas.a: Lionel stops an individual in his secure area who is not wearing a badge.

qas.q:
Which of the following is a best practice for physical security?
(PHYSICAL SECURITY)
qas.a: Report suspicious activity.

qas.q:
Which of the following is an example of two-factor authentication?
(IDENTITY MANAGEMENT)
qas.a: A Common Access Card and Personal Identification Number.

qas.q:
What is the best way to protect your Common Access Card (CAC) or Personal Identity Verification (PIV) card?
(IDENTITY MANAGEMENT)
qas.a: Store it in a shielded sleeve.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true of using a DoD Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) token?
(IDENTITY MANAGEMENT)
qas.a: It should only be in a system while actively using it for a PKI-required task.

qas.q:
What must authorized personnel do before permitting another individual to enter a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
(SENSITIVE COMPARTMENTED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Confirm the individual's need-to-know and access.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true of Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
(SENSITIVE COMPARTMENTED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Access requires Top Secret clearance and indoctrination into the SCI program.

qas.q:
Which of the following is NOT a potential consequence of using removable media unsafely in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
(SENSITIVE COMPARTMENTED INFORMATION)
qas.a: Damage to the removable media.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true of transmitting Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI)?
(SENSITIVE COMPARTMENTED INFORMATION)
qas.a: You many only transport SCI if you have been courier-briefed for SCI.

qas.q:
A compromise of Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI) occurs when a person who does not have the required clearance or access caveats comes into possession of SCI _________.
(SENSITIVE COMPARTMENTED INFORMATION)
qas.a: In any manner.

qas.q:
What portable electronic devices (PEDs) are permitted in a SCIF?
(REMOVABLE MEDIA IN A SCIF)
qas.a: Only expressly authorized government-owned PEDs.

qas.q:
How should you label removable media used in a Sensitive Compartmented Information Facility (SCIF)?
(REMOVABLE MEDIA IN A SCIF)
qas.a: With the maximum classification, date of creation, point of contact, and Change Management (CM) Control Number.

qas.q:
What is the response to an incident such as opening an uncontrolled DVD on a computer in a SCIF?
(REMOVABLE MEDIA IN A SCIF)
qas.a: All of these.

qas.q:
Which of the following is NOT a type of malicious code?
(MALICIOUS CODE)
qas.a: Executables.

qas.q:
Which of the following is a way to prevent the spread of malicious code?
(MALICIOUS CODE)
qas.a: Scan all external files before uploading to your computer.

qas.q:
Which of the following actions can help to protect your identity?
(WEBSITE USE)
qas.a: Shred personal documents.

qas.q:
Which is an appropriate use of government e-mail?
(SOCIAL ENGINEERING)
qas.a: Use a digital signature when sending attachments or hyperlinks.

qas.q:
What type of social engineering targets particular groups of people?
(SOCIAL ENGINEERING)
qas.a: Spear phishing.

qas.q:
What type of social engineering targets senior officials?
(SOCIAL ENGINEERING)
qas.a: Whaling.

qas.q:
How can you protect yourself from social engineering?
(SOCIAL ENGINEERING)
qas.a: Verify the identity of all individuals.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true?
(SOCIAL ENGINEERING)
qas.a: Digitally signed e-mails are more secure.

qas.q:
Which of the following is true of traveling overseas with a mobile phone?
(TRAVEL)
qas.a: A personally owned device approved under Bring Your Own Approved Device (BYOAD) policy must be unenrolled while out of the country.

qas.q:
Which of the following is a concern when using your Government-issued laptop in public?
(TRAVEL)
qas.a: The physical security of the device.

qas.q:
What should Sara do when using publicly available Internet, such as hotel Wi-Fi?
(TRAVEL)
qas.a: Only connect with Government VPN.

qas.q:
What is the danger of using public Wi-Fi connections?
(TRAVEL)
qas.a: Both of these.

qas.q:
Which of the following personally-owned computer peripherals is permitted for use with Government-furnished equipment?
(USE OF GFE)
qas.a: A headset with a microphone through a Universal Serial Bus (USB) port.

qas.q:
How can you protect data on your mobile computing and portable electronic devices (PEDs)?
(MOBILE DEVICES)
qas.a: Enable automatic screen locking after a period of inactivity.

qas.q:
Which of the following is NOT a risk associated with near field communication (NFC)?
(MOBILE DEVICES)
qas.a: Additional data charges.

qas.q:
Which of the following best describes the conditions under which mobile devices and applications can track your location?
(MOBILE DEVICES)
qas.a: It may occur at any time without your knowledge or consent.

qas.q:
Which of the following is an example of removable media?
(MOBILE DEVICES)
qas.a: External hard drive.

qas.q: Which of the following is true of Internet of Things (IoT) devices?
qas.a: They can become an attack vector to other devices on your home network.

qas.q: When is it appropriate to have your security badge visible?
qas.a: At all times when in the facility.

qas.q: What should the owner of this printed SCI do differently?
qas.a: Retrieve classified documents promptly from printers.

qas.q: What should the participants in this conversation involving SCI do differently?
qas.a: Physically assess that everyone within listening distance is cleared and has a need-to-know for the information being discussed.

qas.q: Which of the following demonstrates proper protection of mobile devices?
qas.a: Linda encrypts all of the sensitive data on her government-issued mobile devices.

qas.q:
Which of the following does NOT constitute spillage?
(SPILLAGE)
qas.a: Classified information that should be unclassified and is downgraded.

qas.q:
Which of the following is NOT an appropriate way to protect against inadvertent spillage?
(SPILLAGE)
qas.a: Use the classified network for all work, including unclassified work.

qas.q:
Which of the following may help to prevent spillage?
(SPILLAGE)
qas.a: Follow procedures for transferring data to and from outside agency and non-government networks.

qas.q: Which of the following should you NOT do if you find classified information on the internet?
qas.a: Download the information.
